page: OVHcloud t2-le-180 is a KVM-based server in the Cloud GPU family. It features 60 dedicated physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6226R processors running at 2.9 GHz with a single thread per core. The system is equipped with 180.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.0 GB per core. Hardware acceleration is delivered via four bundled NVIDIA Volta V100S GPUs, providing a total of 128 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is rated at a 10 Gbps baseline.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 60701.4 and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression reaching 324.83 GB/sec. Single-core performance is average, as shown by a geekbench single-core score of 1239.0 and stress-ng single-core score of 1480.7. The server demonstrates top-tier capabilities in LLM inference, achieving 152.1 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 18.53 tokens/sec for text generation on a large 70B model, alongside 119.58 tokens/sec for text generation on a medium 7B model. Memory bandwidth and database operations fall into the average tier.

The hardware profile presents a tradeoff between massive GPU acceleration and moderate per-core system memory. While the 128 GB of total VRAM and dedicated 60-core CPU architecture support parallelized workloads, memory-bound applications requiring large system RAM allocations relative to compute may face constraints. This server is configured for workloads such as large language model inference, parallelized scientific computing, multi-threaded data processing, and GPU-accelerated rendering.
description: OVHcloud t2-le-180 is a KVM-based GPU-accelerated server featuring 60 dedicated Intel Xeon 6226R physical cores operating at 2.9 GHz, paired with 180.0 GB of system memory. The hardware configuration includes four NVIDIA Volta V100S GPUs providing 128 GB of total VRAM, a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and a 10 Gbps network baseline. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier LLM inference speeds, processing 70B model prompts at 152.1 tokens/sec and generating text at 18.53 tokens/sec. Conversely, single-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. The resource allocation presents a tradeoff of high GPU capacity against a moderate 3.0 GB of system RAM per core. This server is suited for parallelized workloads, including large language model inference, multi-threaded data processing, and GPU-accelerated rendering.
og_description: OVHcloud t2-le-180 is a GPU-accelerated server with 60 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 180 GB RAM, and four NVIDIA V100S GPUs, delivering top-tier LLM inference and strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud t2-le-180 features 60 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 180 GB RAM, and four NVIDIA V100S GPUs for top-tier LLM inference workloads.
tagline: A dedicated sixty-core processor platform with four integrated graphics accelerators and high-speed local solid-state storage for parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: 60 dedicated Intel Xeon 6226R physical cores running at 2.9 GHz
Four NVIDIA Volta V100S GPUs with 128 GB total VRAM
180.0 GB system memory and 500 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier LLM inference performance across small, medium, and large models
Strong multi-core CPU performance with average single-core speeds
Suited for parallelized data processing and machine learning inference
